# Developer Experience Checklist: Retro-migration - Version Marker and State-Keyed Backfill/Relocate

**Purpose**: Validate the quality of PRSG-011 developer-experience requirements for operator-safe dry-run/apply output, exact next-command guidance, static scaffold/autopilot Tier-2 suggestions, and Claude Code/Codex skill parity.
**Created**: 2026-06-09
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Dry-Run Output

- [x] CHK001 - Does `migrate-structure.sh --dry-run` require one deterministic report that lists pending Tier-1 repository edits, Tier-0 navigation backfill, skipped frozen specs, planned backup path, and no-op current-state items in stable order? [Completeness, Spec §FR-002; Contract §Output]
- [x] CHK002 - Does `relocate-process-artifacts.sh --dry-run` require one deterministic report with exact proposed PROCESS moves, protected CONTRACT artifacts, dirty-tree/apply-blocked state, generated-link/index updates, stamp decision, collision blocks, and planned backup path? [Completeness, Spec §FR-011; Relocation Contract §Output]
- [x] CHK003 - Are dry-run modes explicitly read-only and safe on dirty trees, so operators can inspect pending work before cleaning their workspace? [Operator Safety, Spec §FR-003; Quickstart §Repository Migration Dry-Run, §Tier-2 Relocation Dry-Run]
- [x] CHK004 - Are no-op states visible for already-current repository migration and already-normalized Tier-2 relocation, rather than leaving operators to infer that nothing changed from an empty item list? [Clarity, Spec §FR-006, §FR-018, §FR-021; Report Contract §Enumerations]

## Apply Output

- [x] CHK005 - Does apply output require the backup path to be printed in the JSON report whenever a pending mutation is applied? [Completeness, Spec §FR-012; Report Contract §Field Semantics]
- [x] CHK006 - Does the shared report vocabulary expose `applied`, `backup`, `generated_update`, `move`, `stamp`, and failure actions so operators can see what changed or where an apply stopped? [Clarity, Spec §FR-021; Report Contract §items[].action]
- [x] CHK007 - Are already-current apply reruns required to report no-op without creating another backup or rewriting files? [Measurability, Spec §FR-006, §FR-018, §SC-007; Contracts §Idempotency]
- [x] CHK008 - Do recovery instructions name the created backup path after post-backup failures, instead of only saying that recovery is possible? [Operator Safety, Spec §FR-021; Quickstart §Recovery]

## Exact Safe Next Commands

- [x] CHK009 - Does the feature spec require `speckit-upgrade` to tell operators the exact safe next repository migration command, including a dry-run first command and the clean-tree apply follow-up, rather than leaving this only as a plan implementation note? [Resolved, Completeness, Spec §FR-028; Plan §Increment 2; Quickstart §Expected Skill Guidance] - `FR-028` now requires the exact dry-run/apply command sequence and clean-tree apply precondition.
- [x] CHK010 - Do scaffold/autopilot requirements require the exact Tier-2 dry-run command and clean-tree apply follow-up when a thawed legacy spec has relocatable PROCESS artifacts? [Completeness, Spec §US3 AS1, §FR-026; Data Model §OperatorSuggestion]
- [x] CHK011 - Are frozen/in-flight, already-current, no-candidate, and out-of-scope cases required to suppress Tier-2 suggestions or report why no action is needed? [Clarity, Spec §US3 AS2-AS3, §FR-027; Data Model §OperatorSuggestion]
- [x] CHK012 - Is the safe command order clear to operators: repository migration first, then explicit per-spec Tier-2 relocation only for thawed legacy specs? [Sequence, Spec §FR-022; Plan §Implementation Increments]

## Static Suggestions and Non-Automation

- [x] CHK013 - Are scaffold and autopilot flows prohibited from executing either `relocate-process-artifacts.sh --dry-run` or `relocate-process-artifacts.sh --apply` automatically? [Operator Safety, Spec §FR-020, §FR-026; Research §Decision 6]
- [x] CHK014 - Does the data model carry `auto_executed: always false` for operator suggestions so tests can assert suggestion-only behavior? [Measurability, Data Model §OperatorSuggestion]
- [x] CHK015 - Do success criteria require 100% suggestion coverage for thawed legacy specs with relocatable PROCESS artifacts and 0 automatic codemod executions? [Measurability, Spec §SC-006]
- [x] CHK016 - Does the plan require Layer 3 fixtures for scaffold/autopilot suggestion behavior, including no suggestions for out-of-scope namespaces? [Coverage, Plan §Increment 2; Quickstart §Focused Verification]

## Claude Code and Codex Parity

- [x] CHK017 - Does PRSG-011 make Claude Code/Codex skill parity a requirement for every changed `speckit-upgrade`, `speckit-scaffold-spec`, and `speckit-autopilot` operator-facing wording surface, rather than relying only on the roadmap cross-cutting rule and declared file list? [Resolved, Completeness, Spec §FR-029, §SC-008; Plan §Increment 2; Quickstart §Expected Skill Guidance] - `FR-029` and `SC-008` now require behavior-equivalent mirror wording and parity verification.
- [x] CHK018 - Does the plan list both Claude Code and Codex mirrors for all three affected skills and the autopilot phase-execution reference? [Traceability, Plan §Declared File Operations, §Project Structure]
- [x] CHK019 - Does planned verification include Layer 8 parity checks for mirrored Codex skill prose? [Coverage, Plan §Technical Context, §Increment 2; Quickstart §Focused Verification]
- [x] CHK020 - Are shared deterministic scripts and contracts kept runtime-agnostic single copies, rather than duplicated separately for Claude Code and Codex? [Consistency, Plan §Structure Decision; Roadmap §Cross-cutting requirements]

## Rerun Verification

- [x] CHK021 - Does `speckit-upgrade` now have a spec-level exact dry-run/apply command requirement and quickstart guidance? [Spec §FR-028; Data Model §UpgradeMigrationGuidance; Quickstart §Expected Skill Guidance]
- [x] CHK022 - Do scaffold/autopilot Tier-2 suggestions now pin the exact dry-run/apply command templates in spec and data model artifacts? [Spec §FR-026; Data Model §OperatorSuggestion]
- [x] CHK023 - Is Claude Code/Codex behavior-equivalent parity now a PRSG-011 feature requirement with Layer 3 and Layer 8 verification coverage? [Spec §FR-029, §SC-008; Plan §Increment 2; Quickstart §Focused Verification]
- [x] CHK024 - Do developer-experience artifacts now require exact safe-command sequencing without allowing scaffold/autopilot or upgrade flows to auto-run Tier-2 relocation? [Spec §FR-020, §FR-026, §FR-028; Quickstart §Expected Skill Guidance]

## Notes

- Initial developer-experience run found two gap markers: one for `speckit-upgrade` exact repository migration next-command requirements, and one for making PRSG-011 Claude Code/Codex skill parity an explicit feature requirement.
- Both gaps were resolved by tightening `spec.md`, `plan.md`, `data-model.md`, and `quickstart.md` with exact command strings, safe-command sequencing, Layer 3/Layer 8 verification coverage, and behavior-equivalent Claude Code/Codex mirror requirements.
- Rerun verification added CHK021-CHK024 and found no new gaps.
